{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tokushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.higashimiyoshi.lg.jp/docs/873.html Higashimiyoshi Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.tokushima.lg.jp/rekishiru/remains/5023145 Tokushima Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tokushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.higashimiyoshi.lg.jp/docs/873.html Higashimiyoshi Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.tokushima.lg.jp/rekishiru/remains/5023145 Tokushima Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tokushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.higashimiyoshi.lg.jp/docs/873.html Higashimiyoshi Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.tokushima.lg.jp/rekishiru/remains/5023145 Tokushima Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tokushima)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Use mdy dates|date=July 2022}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | name = Tanda Kofun | native_name =丹田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tokushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanda Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E|region:JP-36|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]], Japan | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|1|26.25|N|133|56|9.98|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|''' Tanda Kofun'''|丹田古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nishisho neighborhood of the town of [[Higashimiyoshi, Tokushima]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in Japan. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139587|title=丹田古墳 |trans-title= Tanda Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanda Kofun is located on a ridge of 460-meter Mount Kamo, on the south bank of the middle reaches of the [[Yoshino River]] in western Tokushima Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tokushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.higashimiyoshi.lg.jp/docs/873.html Higashimiyoshi Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.tokushima.lg.jp/rekishiru/remains/5023145 Tokushima Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}
Tanda Kofun

It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was originally believed to be an ancient [[sutra mound]] and had a small chapel to [[Kūkai]] on its summit. It was found to be an ancient ''kofun'' by the town's cultural properties committee in 1958. It is unusual in that the tumulus is a pile of crystalline [[schist]] stone rubble, with no soil used in its construction. It has a total length of 37 meters, orientated to the west. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber in the posterior circular portion, and measures 4.51 meters in length, 1.3 meters in width (eastern end), 1.28 meters (western end), and 1.2 meters in height. An [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted in 1969 by [[Doshisha University]], and [[grave goods]], including one [[bronze mirror]], an iron sword and an iron ax, form the early Kofun period were found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> A group of [[pit dwelling]]s from the end of the [[Yayoi period]] was found at the nearby Kamo Higashihara site, and it is presumed that the village was involved in the construction of the Tanda Kofun.
The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[Awa-Kamo Station]] on the [[JR Shikoku]] [[Tokushima Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 37 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 17.5 meter diameter x 3 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 6.6 meters wide x 1 meter high ; Connecting restriction: 10 meters wide
